text_fr: libres  de  decider  elles-memes  s il  est  de  l'inten~t  de leurs  travaux  que  l'Espagne y  participe,
text_en: free to decide themselves if it is in their work that Spain participates in it,

text_fr: Decide
text_en: Decision

items_fr: 1. D'abroger la clause de la resolution 39 (I) adoptee le 12  decembre  1946  par  l'Assemblee  generale,  aux termes de laquelle l'Assemblee recommandait aux Etats Membres  de  rappeler  de  Madrid  leurs  ambassadeurs et ministres ;

2. D'abroger la  recommandation  visaint  a empecher l'Espagne d'adherer a  des  institutions  internationales etablies  par Jes  Nations  Unies  OU  reliees  a  !'Organisa› tion,  recommandation  qui  figure  clans  la  meme  resolu› tion adoptee par l'Assemblee generale en 1946 et relative aux relations  entre  les  Membres  des  Nations  Unies  et l'Espagne.
items_en: 1. To repeal the clause of Resolution 39 (I) adopted by the General Assembly on 12 December 1946, according to which the Assembly recommended that Member States remind their ambassadors and ministers from Madrid;

2. To repeal the recommendation to prevent Spain from acceding to international institutions established by the United Nations OR linked to the Organization, a recommendation which appears in the same resolution adopted by the General Assembly in 1946 and relating to relations between the Members of the United Nations and Spain.

text_fr: 304eme seance  pleniere,
text_en: 304th plenary session,

text_fr: le  4  novembre 1950.
text_en: November 4, 1950.

text_fr: 387  (V).  Libye:  rapport  du  Commissaire  des Nations  Unies  en  Libye;  rapports  des  Puis› sances administrantes de la Libye
text_en: 387 (V). Libya: report of the United Nations Commissioner for Libya; reports of Libya's administering powers

text_fr: L' Assemblee  generale,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Ayant  decide, par  sa  resolution  289  A  (1 V)  du 21 novembre  1949,  que  la  Libye  serait  constituee  en un Etat uni,  independant  et  souverain,
text_en: Having decided, by its resolution 289 A (1 V) of 21 November 1949, that Libya would be a united, independent and sovereign State,

text_fr: Ayant pris  acte du  rapport du Commissaire des  Na› tions  Unies  en  Libye 2 , etabli  en  consultation  avec  le Conseil  pour  la  Libye,  et  des  rapports  des  Puissances administrantes3,  presentes  a  I' Assemblee  generale  con› formement  a  la  resolution  289  A  (IV), ainsi  que  des declarations 4 faites  par  le  Commissaire  <les  Nations Unies  et  par  les  representants  du  Conseil  pour  la Libye,
text_en: Having taken note of the report of the Commissioner of the United Nations in Libya 2 , prepared in consultation with the Council for Libya, and of the reports of the administering Powers3, submitted to the General Assembly in accordance with resolution 289 A (IV), and of the statements 4 made by the Commissioner of the United Nations and the representatives of the Council for Libya,

text_fr: Ayant  note  en  particulier que  le  Commissaire  <les Nations  Unies  a  exprime  le  ferme  espoir  de  voir  le but que vise  l' Assemblee  generale,  c'est-a-dire la  cons› titution  de  la  Libye  en  un  Etat independant  et  souve› rain,  atteint  clans  les  delais  impartis,  moyennant  la collaboration  croissante  des  Puissances  administrantes avec  le  Commissaire  des  Nations Unies et la coordina› tion  de  leurs  initiatives  clans  ce  sens,
text_en: Having noted in particular that the Commissioner <the United Nations has expressed the firm hope of seeing the goal of the General Assembly, i.e. the establishment of Libya as an independent and renewed State, achieved in the prescribed time, through the increasing cooperation of the administering Powers with the Commissioner of the United Nations and the coordination of their initiatives in this sense,

text_fr: Ayant  pris  acte de  ce  que  le  Commissaire  des  Na› tions  Unies  a  declare,  clans  le  rapport  en  question, qu'il faudrait  apporter a  la  Libye  une  assistance  tech·› nique  et  financiere,  non  seulement  avant,  mais  encore apres  son  accession  a  l'independance, si  le  Gouverne› ment libyen  demande une  aide  de  ce  genre,
text_en: Having taken note of the statement by the Commissioner of the United Nations, in the report in question, that technical and financial assistance should be provided to Libya, not only before but also after independence, if the Libyan Government requests such assistance,

items_fr: 1. Exprime le  fenne  espoir que  le  Commissaire des Nations Unies en Libye,  aide et conseille  par les  mem› bres  du  Conseil  pour  la  Libye,  prendra  Jes  mesures n~cessaires  pour s'acquitter de  ses  fonctions  en  ce  qui concerne  la  realisation  de  l'independance et  de  !'unite de  la Libye conformement a  la resolution precitee;
2. Invite les  autorites  interessees  a  prendre  toutes les mesures  necessaires  pour  assurer  !'application
items_en: 1. Expresses the hope that the United Nations Commissioner in Libya, assisted and advised by the Council for Libya, will take the necessary steps to carry out his duties with regard to the realization of independence and unity of Libya in accordance with the resolution;
2. Invites interested authorities to take all necessary measures to ensure!'

text_fr: rnpide,  integrale  et  effica,:c  de  la  resolution  du  21  110·:eic,brc  1949,  notamn,ent  pour  realiser  !'unite  de  la Libve  et  transferer  les  pouvoin,  a un  Gouvernement libye11  independant; et,  er,  outre,
text_en: nrpid, integral and effective:c of the resolution of 21 110·:eic,brc 1949,notamn, to achieve the unity of the Libve and to transfer the povoine, to an independent libye11 Government; and, in addition,

text_fr: 3. Recommande
text_en: 3. Recommends

items_fr: u) Qu'une Assemblee  nationale  dumt::nt  repr~senta› ti ve des  habitants  de  la  Libye  soit  convoquee  le  plus tot  possible,  et  en  tout  cas  le  ler  janvier  1951;
b)  Que cette  A~sembke  natinnale  constitue  aussitot qne  1,ossihle  un  Gouverriement  provisoire  de  la  Libye, en  prenant comme  objecti f la  date  du  ler avril  1951 ;

c I Que  les  Puissances  administrantes  transferent gra,luellement  leurs  pouvoirs  au  Gouvernement  provi› soire,  de  fac;on  que  tous  Jes  ponvoirs  qu'elles exercent ·1ctm·Ilement  i;oient  tra11sfrres.  d'ici  au  ler  janvier 1952.  a11  Gouvernement dt1ment  constitue  de  la  Libye;

d J Qur  le  Cornmissaire  des  Nations  Unies,  a:de  et conseill?  par  Jes  membres  du  Conseil  pour la Libye, arri~te  immediatement,  en  collaboration  avec  les  Puis› sances  administra11tes,  les  rnodalites  dn  transfert  de pouvoirs  prevu  ci-dessus  a  l'alinea C;

4. lwvfre instamment le Conseil economique  et social,  Jes  institutions specialisees  et le  Secretaire gene› ral  de  l'Organisation  des  Nations  Unies  a  fournir  a la  Libye,  dans  tuute  la  mesure de  lettrs  moyens,  !'assis› tance  technique  et  financiere  qu'elle pourra  demander pom  crec'r Jes  conditions  necessaires an  progres econo› mique  et  social  du  pays;

5. Recommandc d nouTeau que.\_. lorsqu'elle  sera constituee  en  Etat  independant.  la  Libye  soit  admise comme  l\Tembre  de  !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies, conformement a !'Article 4  de  la  Charte.
items_en: (u) That a National Assembly should be convened as soon as possible, and in any event on 1 January 1951;
(b) That this natinal A~sembke also constitutes a provisional Government of Libya, taking as its object the date of 1 April 1951;
(c) The administering Powers should transfer their powers to the Government of the Republic of Libya free of charge by the day of 1 January 1952.

d J Qur the Secretary-General of the United Nations, has and advises by my members of the Council for Libya, immediately after, in collaboration with the Administration, the rnodalites dn the transfer of powers provided for above to the alinea C;

4. the Economic and Social Council, the specialized institutions and the Secretary-General of the United Nations to provide Libya, in so far as possible with the means, with technical and financial assistance that it may request the necessary conditions in the economic and social progress of the country;
5. Recommendation of nouTeau that.\_. when it will be constituted in independent State. Libya is admitted as the member of the United Nations, in accordance with Article 4 of the Charter.

text_fr: 307eme seance  pleniere,
text_en: 307th plenary session,

text_fr: le  17 noveml>re  1950.
text_en: the 17 noveml>re 1950.

text_fr: 388 ( V).  Dispositions  economiques  et  finan· cieres  relatives a la  Libye
text_en: 388 ( V). Economic and financial provisions relating to Libya

text_fr: A
text_en: A

text_fr: .·lttendu qu'en  conformite  des  dispositions  de  ]'ar› ticle  23  et  Ju  paragraphe  3  de  l'annexe XI du Traite de  paix avec  l'Italie, la  question  du  sort  des  anciennes colonies  italiennes  a  ete  soumise  le  15  septembre  1948 a  I' Assemblee  generale  par  Jes  Gouvernements  de  la France,  de  !'Union des  Republiques  socidistes  sovie› tiqnes,  du  Royaume-Uni  de  Grande-Bretagne  et  d'Jr.· lande  du  Nord  et  des  Etats-l.:nis  d'Amerique,
text_en: Whereas, in accordance with the provisions of Article 23 and Article 3 of Annex XI to the Treaty of Peace with Italy, the question of the fate of the former Italian colonies was submitted on 15 September 1948 to the General Assembly by the Governments of France, the Union of Soviet Societal Republics, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Germany and the States of America,

text_fr: Attendu qu'en  vertu  des  dispositions  precitees,  Jes quatre  Puissances  ont  convenu  d'accepter  la  recom› mandation  de  l'Assemblee generale  et  de  prendre  Jes mesnres  appropriees  pour la  mettre a execution,
text_en: Whereas by virtue of the precise provisions, Is four Powers have agreed to accept the recom› mandate of the General Assembly and to take appropriate jes mesnres to implement it,

text_fr: Attendtt que  l' Assemblee  generale,  par  ses  resolu·· tions  du  21  novembre  1949  et  du  17  novembre  19501\ a  recommande que l'independance de la Libye devienne
text_en: Whereas the General Assembly, by its resolutions of 21 November 1949 and 17 November 1950, recommended that Libya's independence should become

text_fr: effective  I~  plus  tot  possible,  et au  plus tard le  ler jan› vier  1952,
text_en: As soon as possible, and no later than 1 January 1952,

text_fr: Attendu que  le  paragraphe  19  de  !'annexe XIV du Traite de  paix avec  I'Italie, qui  contient Jes  dispositions economiques  et  financieres  relatives  aux  territoires cedes,  porte  que:  "Les  dispositions  de  la  presente annexe ne seront pas applicables aux anciennes colonies italiennes.  Les  dispositions  economiques  et  financieres qui  leur  seront  appliquees  seront  incluses  clans  Jes arrangements qui, aux termes de !'article 23  du  present traite,  regleront  le  sort  de  ces  territoires",
text_en: Whereas paragraph 19 of Annex XIV to the Treaty of Peace with Italy, which contains economic and financial provisions relating to the territories concerned, states that: "The provisions of this Annex shall not apply to the former Italian colonies; the economic and financial provisions to be applied to them shall be included in the arrangements which, pursuant to Article 23 of this Treaty, shall regulate the fate of those territories",

text_fr: Attendu qu'il est souhaitable que les dispositions eco› nomiques  et  financieres  concernant  la  Libye  soient arretees  avant  que  n'ait lieu  le  transfert  de  pouvoirs sur  ce  territoire  afin  qu'elles  puissent  etre  appliquees aussitot  que  possible,
text_en: Whereas it is desirable that the economic and financial provisions concerning Libya be stopped before the transfer of powers in that territory takes place so that they can be applied as soon as possible,

text_fr: L' Assemblee  generate Approuve les  articles  suivants:
text_en: The General Assembly approves the following articles:

text_fr: Article  premier
text_en: Article 1

items_fr: 1. La  Libye  recevra,  sans  paiement,  les biens meub!es  et immeubles  situes  en  Libye  dont le  proprie› taire  est  l'Etat italien,  en  son  nom  propre  ou  au  nom de  !'administration italienne  de  la  Libye.
2. Seront immediatement  transferes:

a) Les biens  constituant le  domaine public  ( demanio pubblico) et  le patrimoine  indisponible (patrimonio indisponibile) de  l'Etat en  Libye,  ainsi  que les archives et les  documents  appropries  de  caractere  administratif OU  d'interet technique  concernant  la  Libye  OU  se  rap› portant a des  biens  dont  le  transfert est  prevu  par  la presente  resolution;
b) Les biens du parti fasciste  et de  ses  organisations en Libye.
3. Seront  transferes  par  slfrcroit  et aux  conditions a fixer  par accord  special  entre  l'Italie et la  Libye :

a) Les  biens  disponibles  de  I'Etat (patrimonio  dis› ponibile) et  les  biens  appartenant  aux  agences  auto› nomes  de  l'Etat  ( aziende  autonome), les  uns  et  les autres  situes  en  Libye;
b) Les  droits  de  l'Etat sur  les  capitaux  et  3ur  Jes biens  des  etablissements,  societes  et  associations  de caractere  public  situes  en  Libye.

4. Lorsque l'activite desdits  etablissements,  societes et associations  s'etend a I'Italie OU a d'autres pays  que la Libye,  la  Libye  recevra  uniquement  les  droits  de l'Etat  italien  ou  de  !'administration  italienne  de  la Libye  qui  ne  concernent  que  leur  activite  en  Libye. Dans  les  cas  ou  J'Etat italien  ou  !'administration ita› lienne  de  la  Libye  n'avaient clans  ces  etablissements, societes  et  associations  que  des  fonctions  de  direction, la  Libye  ne  pourra  pretendre  a  aucun  droit  clans  ces organismes.

5. Qu'elle en  soit  proprietaire  en  son  nom  propre ou  au  nom  de  !'administration italienne  de  la  Libye, l'Italie gardera  la  propriete  des  immeubles  necessaires au fonctionnement de  ses services diplomatiques et con› sulaires  et  des  institutions  scolaires  necessaires  a  la communaute  italienne  actuelle  la  ou  les  conditions
items_en: 1. Libya shall receive, without payment, the goods and immovables situated in Libya, the property of which is the Italian State, in its own name or in the name of the Italian administration of Libya.
2. The following shall be transferred immediately:

(a) The property constituting the public domain (demanio pubblico) and the unavailability (patrimonio indisponibile) of the State in Libya, as well as the archives and appropriate documents of administrative character OR of technical interest concerning Libya OR relating to property the transfer of which is provided for in this resolution;
(b) The property of the fascist party and its organizations in Libya.
3. Will be transferred by slfrcroit and subject to the conditions to be laid down by special agreement between Italy and Libya:
(a) The assets available from the State (patrimonio dis› ponibile) and the assets belonging to the State's self-employed agencies (autonomous aziend), both located in Libya;
(b) The State's rights over capital and 3rd property of public establishments, companies and associations located in Libya.

4. When the activities of such establishments, societies and associations extend to Italy OR to countries other than Libya, Libya shall receive only the rights of the Italian State or of the Italian administration of Libya which concern only their activities in Libya. In cases where I Italian State or the Italian administration of Libya had only those establishments, societies and associations which are responsible for management functions, Libya shall not be able to grant any rights to these bodies.

5. Whether it owns it in its own name or in the name of the Italian administration of Libya, Italy will retain the property of the buildings necessary for the operation of its diplomatic and consular services and the educational institutions necessary for the present Italian community on the terms or conditions

text_fr: !'exigent.  Des  accords  speciaux  interviendront  entre l'Italie  et  la Libye  pour  la determination  de ces immeubles.
text_en: Specific agreements will be reached between Italy and Libya for the determination of these buildings.

items_fr: 6. Les  edifices  destines  aux  cultes  non  musulmans et  Ieurs  dependances  seront  transferes  par l'Italie aux communautes  religieuses  respectives.
7. Des  accords  speciaux  pourront  intervenir  entre l'Italie et  la  Libye  pour assurer le  fonctionnement  des hopitaux  en  Libye.
items_en: 6. The buildings intended for non-Muslim and dependent religions will be transferred by Italy to the respective religious communities.
7. Special agreements between Italy and Libya may be reached to ensure the functioning of hospitals in Libya.

text_fr: Article  II
text_en: Article II

text_fr: L'Italie et  la  Libye  regleront  par  accords  speciaux les  conditions  clans  Iesquelles  seront  transferees a des organisations  analogues  clans  l'Etat libyen  Jes  obliga› tions  des  organisations  italiennes  publiques  ou  privees pour  la  securite  sociale  a  I'egard  des  habitants  de  la Libye,  ainsi  que  la  part  proportionnelle  des  reserves constituees  par  ces  organisations.  Cette  part  des  re› serves  sera  prelevee,  de  preference,  sur les  biens  fon› ciers  et  Jes immobilisations  de  ces  organisations  en Libye.
text_en: Italy and Libya will regulate by special agreements the conditions which will be transferred to similar organisations in the Libyan State by public or private Italian organisations for social security in the case of the inhabitants of Libya, as well as the proportional share of the reserves made up by these organisations.

text_fr: Article  III
text_en: Article III

text_fr: L'Italie  restera  tenue  d'assurer  le  paiement  des pensions civiles  ou militaires acquises a la  date  d'entree en  vigueur  du  Traite de  paix avec  l'Italie et dont elle etait debitrice a cette  date.  Cette obligation s'etend aux droits  a  pension  non  encore  echus.  L'Italie et la  Libye regleront par des arrangements Jes  conditions clans Ies› quelles  l'Italie s'acquittera de  cette  obligation.
text_en: Italy will continue to be obliged to ensure payment of the civil or military pensions acquired on the date of entry into force of the Peace Treaty with Italy and of which it was responsible on that date. This obligation extends to pension rights not yet due. Italy and Libya will settle by arrangements Is conditional upon which Italy will fulfil this obligation.

text_fr: Article  IV
text_en: Article IV

text_fr: La Libye sera exemptee du paiement de  toute partie de  la  dette  publique  italienne.
text_en: Libya will be exempt from payment of any part of the Italian public debt.

text_fr: Article  V
text_en: Article V

text_fr: L'Italie restituera,  clans  le  plus  bref  delai, a Ieurs proprietaires,  tous  Jes  bateaux  detenus  par elle  ou  par ses  ressortissants  et  qui  seront  prouves  avoir  ete  la propriete  d'anciens  ressortissants  italiens  de  Libye  OU avoir  ete  enregistres  en  Libye,  sauf  s'il  s'agit  de bateaux qu'elle ou  ses  ressortissants auraient acquis. de bonne  foi.
text_en: Italy will return, clans the shortest delay, to their owners, all ships owned by it or by its nationals and which will be proved to have been the owner of former Italian nationals of Libya OR to have been registered in Libya, unless they are ships which it or its nationals would have acquired. in good faith.

text_fr: Article  VI
text_en: Article VI

items_fr: 1. Les  biens,  droits  et  interets  des  ressortissants italiens,  y  compris  les  personnes  morales  italiennes,  en Libye,  seront  respectes,  a  condition  qu'ils  aient  ete legalement  acquis.  Ils  ne  seront pas traites  moins  favo› rablement  que  les  biens,  droits  et  interets  des  autres ressortissants etrangers, y compris Jes personnes morales  de  nationalite  etrangere.

2. Les  ressortissants  italiens  qui  quittent  la  Libye pour  s'etablir en  Italie  OU  qui  s'y sont  etablis  depuis le  3  septembre 1943  seront autorises a vendre librement Ieurs  biens  meubles  et  immeubles,  realiser  leurs  actifs et  en  disposer  et,  apres  acquittement  des  dettes  ou impositions  dont  ils pourraient  etre  redevables  en Libye,  a  emporter Ieurs  biens  meubles  et transferer les fonds  qu'ils  possedent,  a  moins  que  ces  biens  et  ces fonds  n'aient ete  illegalement  acquis.  Le  transfert  de
items_en: 1. The property, rights and interests of Italian nationals, including Italian legal persons, in Libya, shall be respected, provided that they have also been acquired. They shall not be treated less favourably than the property, rights and interests of other foreign nationals, including Is legal persons of foreign nationalite.

2. Italian nationals who leave Libya to settle in Italy OR who have been established there since 3 September 1943 will be allowed to freely sell their movable and immovable property, to realise and dispose of their assets and, after payment of debts or taxes for which they may be liable in Libya, to take away their movable property and to transfer the funds they possess, unless these assets and funds have been illegally acquired.

text_fr: ces  biens  ne sera frappe  d'aucun droit d'importation ou d'exportation.
text_en: These goods will not be subject to any import or export duties.

text_fr: Les  conditions  dans  lesquelles  s'effectuera le  trans› fert  en  Italie  de  ces  biens  meubles  seront  fixees  par ac::ord  entre,  d'une part, les  Puissances administrantes ou le  Gouvernement de la Libye lorsqu'il aura ete cons› titue,  et  d'autre part,  le  Gouvernement  de  l'Italie. Les conditions E:t delais  dans  lesquels  s'effectuera le  trans› fert des  fonds,  y  compris  le  produit des  operations  ci› dessus mentionnees, seront egalement fixes  par accord.
text_en: The conditions under which the transfer of such movable property will take place in Italy shall be fixed by agreement between the administering Powers or the Government of Libya once it has been established, and the Government of Italy. The conditions E:t de lois in which the transfer of funds will take place, including the proceeds of the operations mentioned above, shall also be fixed by agreement.

items_fr: 3. Les  societes  constituees  conformement a la  legis› lation italienne  et  dont le  siege  social  est  situe en  Italie seront' traitees  conformement aux dispositions du para› graphe 2  ci-dessus.  Les  societes  constituees  conforme› ment a la legislation italienne et dont le  siege  social  est situe  en  Libye,  et  qui  desirent  transferer  leur  siege social en Italie,  seront egalement traitees conformement aux dispositions  du  paragraphe  ci-dessus, a condition que plus de SO pour 100  du capital de  la  societe  appar› tienne a des  personnes residant normalement en  dehors de  la  Libye  et a condition  que  la  societe  exerce  son activite  en majeure partie  hors  de  la  Libye.

4. Les biens,  droits et inten~ts  existant en  Italie  des anciens  ressortissants  italiens  de  Libye,  ainsi  que  ceux des  societes  precedemment  constituees  conformement a la  legislation  italienne,  ayant  leur  siege  social  en Libye, seront respectes par l'Italie dans la  meme mesure que  les  biens,  droits  et  interets  des  ressortissants  et societes  etrangers  en  general.  Ces  personnes  et  ces societes seront autorisees a effectuer  le  transfert et  la liquidation  de  leurs  biens,  droits  et  interets  dans  Jes memes conditions que  celles  qui  pourront etre µrevue., en  vertu  du  paragraphe  2  ci-dessus.

5. Ni  les  dettes  des  personnes  residant  en  Italic envers  des  personnes  residant  en  Libye  ni  celles  des personnes residant en  Libye envers des  personnes resi› dant  en  Italie  ne  seront  affectees  par  le  transfert  de souverainete.  Le  Gouvernement  de  l'Italie et  les  Puis› sances administrantes,  ou  le  Gouvernement de  la  Libve lorsqu'il aura  ete  constitue,  devront  faciliter  le  regle› ment  de  ces  dettes.  Aux  fins  du  present  paragraphe, le  terme  "personae"  comprend  les  personnes  morales.
items_en: 3. Companies which are incorporated in accordance with the Italian legislation and whose social seat is situated in Italy shall be treated in accordance with the provisions of paragraph 2 above. Companies which are incorporated in accordance with Italian legislation and whose social seat is situated in Libya and which are required to transfer their social seat to Italy shall also be treated in accordance with the provisions of the above paragraph, provided that more than 100 per cent of the capital of the company is held by persons who normally reside outside Libya and provided that the company carries on its business mainly outside Libya.

4. The property, rights and interests existing in Italy of former Italian nationals of Libya, as well as those of companies previously constituted in accordance with Italian legislation, having their social seat in Libya, shall be respected by Italy to the same extent as the property, rights and interests of foreign nationals and companies in general. These persons and companies shall be authorized to effect the transfer and liquidation of their property, rights and interests in the same conditions as those which may be reviewed under paragraph 2 above.
5. Neither the debts of persons residing in Italic to persons residing in Libya nor those of persons residing in Libya to persons residing in Italy shall be affected by the transfer of sovereignty.

text_fr: Article  VII
text_en: Article VII

text_fr: Les biens,  droits  et interets en  Libye qui  sont encore l'objet,  en  consequence  de  la  guerre,  de  mesures  de saisie,  d'administration forcee  ou  de  sequestre,  seront restitues  a  leurs  proprietaires,  le  cas  echeant  a  la  suite de  decisions  du  tribunal  prevu a l'artich~ X  de  la  pre› sente  resolution,  la  OU  ce  tribunal  serait  saisi.
text_en: The property, rights and interests in Libya which are still subject, as a result of the war, to seizure, forcible administration or sequestration, shall be returned to their owners, in the event of decisions of the court provided for in Artich~X of the first resolution, the OR court shall be seized.

text_fr: Article  VII I
text_en: Article VII I

text_fr: Les anciens ressortissants italiens de  Libye continue› rant de jouir de tous les droits de propriete industrielle, litteraire  et  artistique  en  Italie  auxquels  ils  pouvaient pretendre  sous  le  regime  de  la  legislation  en  vigueur lors  de  l'entree  en  vigueur  du  Traite  de  paix.  En attendant  que  la  Libye  adhere  aux  conventions  inter› nationales regissant ces matieres, les  droits de  propriete industrielle,  litteraire  et  artistique  qui  existaient  en Libye sous le  regime des lois italiennes seront respectes pendant la  periode  durant laquelle  ils  seraient restes en vigueur  sous  ce  regime.
text_en: The former Italian citizens of Libya continue to enjoy all the industrial, literary and artistic property rights in Italy to which they were entitled under the legislation in force during the entry into force of the Peace Treaty. Pending Libya's accession to the inter-national conventions regulating these matters, the industrial, literary and artistic property rights which existed in Libya under the regime of Italian laws will be respected during the period during which they would remain in force under this regime.

text_fr: Article  IX
text_en: Article IX

text_fr: Les  dispositions  speciales  suivantes  regleront  le regime  des concessions :
text_en: The following special provisions will regulate the regime of concessions:

items_fr: 1. n. ne  sera  pas  porte  atteinte  aux  concessions accordees sur le  territoire de  la  Libye  par J'Etat italien ou  l'administration italienne  de  la  Libye,  ni  aux  con› trats  de concession (patti  colonici) existant  entre I'Ente  per  la  Colonizzazione  della  Libia ou l'Istituto della  Previdenza  Sociale d'une part, et les  concession› naires  des  terres  objets  de  ces  contrats.,  d'autre part, a moins  qu'il ne  soit  etabli  que  le  concessionnaire  ne s'est  pas  conforme  aux  conditions  essentielles  de  la concession.

2. Seront transferees  immediatement a la  Libye  les terres  qui  avaient  ete  mises  par  l'Italie OU  par !'admi› nistration  italienne  de  la  Libye a la  disposition  de 1' Ente per la  C olonizzazione delta  Libia et de  la gestion de  colonisation  de  I' I stituto  della  Previdenza  S ociale, et  qui  n'ont  pas  encore  fait  l'objet  d'un  contrat  de concession.

3. Seront  transferes a la  Libye,  conformement  au reglement a intervenir  aux  termes  de  l'alinea d du paragaphe 4  ci-apres,  les  terres,  les  batiments  et  leurs dependances  vises  andit  alinea.
4. Des  accords  speciaux  conclus  entre  l'Ttalie et  la Libye  regleront:

a)  La  liquidation  de l'Ente  per  la  Colonizzazione delta  Libia et  de  la  gestion  de  colonisation  de  1' lsti› tuto  della  Previdenza  Sociale, le regime  interimaire desdites institutions  propre a leur permettre de  remplir leurs obligations a l'egard des  concessionnaires dont les contrats  sont  en  cours,  et,  le  cas  echeant,  la  prise  en charge de leurs  fonctions  par de  nouveaux organismes;

b) Le remboursement par ces  institutions des  quote› parts  aux  etablissements  financiers  qui  ont  participe a la  fondation de 1' Ente per la  Colonizzazione delta  Libia., et,  dans  le  cas  de  I' lstituto  delta  Previ,denza  Sociale, la  reintegration  de  la  partie  de  ses  reserves  investie dans  la  gestion  de  colonisation ;
c) La  devolution a la  Libye  de  l'actif residue!  des institutions  mises  en  liquidation;

d) Les  questions  relatives  aux  terres  mises a la disposition  desdites  institutions,  aux  batiments  et a Ieurs  dependances,  qui, a la  suite  de  l~ur  abandon definitif  par  Jes  concessionnaires,  ne  pourraient  faire I'objet de  nouvelles  mises  de  fonds  de  la  part  de  ces institutions ;
e) Le  service  de  l'amortissement des  dettes  de  con› cessionnaires  envers  ces  institutions.

5. En  raison  de  la  renonciation  par  le  Gouverne› ment  italien a ses creances  envers  ces  institutions, celles-ci  procederont a l'annulation des  dettes  des  con› cessionnaires  et a la  radiation  des  hypotheques  qui  Jes garantissent.
items_en: 1. n. shall not affect the concessions granted in the territory of Libya by the Italian State or the Italian administration of Libya, or the concession contracts (patti colonici) existing between the Ente per la Colonizzazione della Libya or the Istituto della Previdenza Sociale on the one hand, and the concession contracts of the land subject to those contracts., on the other hand, unless it is established that the concessionaire has not complied with the essential conditions of the concession.

2. The lands which had been set up by Italy OR by Italian administration of Libya shall be immediately transferred to Libya to the disposal of the Ente per la C olonizzazione delta Libya and the management of colonization of the I' institutiono della Previdenza S ociale, and which have not yet been the subject of a concession contract.
3. Will be transferred to Libya, in accordance with the regulations to be applied under the alinea d of paragaph 4 below, the lands, buildings and their dependents aimed andit alinea.
4. Special agreements between Italy and Libya will regulate:

(a) The liquidation of the Ente per la Colonizzazione delta Libya and the settlement management of l'lsti› tuto della Previdenza Sociale, the interim regime of the said institutions shall enable them to fulfil their obligations with regard to the concessionaires whose contracts are under way, and, in the event thereof, the taking over of their functions by new bodies;

(b) Reimbursement by these institutions of the shares to the financial institutions which have participated in the foundation of the Ente per la Colonizzazione delta Libya, and, in the case of l'lstituto delta Previ,denza Sociale, the reintegration of the part of its reserves invested in the management of colonization;
(c) Libya's devolution of the remaining assets of the liquidated institutions;

(d) Matters relating to the lands made available to the said institutions, buildings and dependencies, which, following the abandonment by the concessionaires, could not be remitted by these institutions;
(e) The amortization service for the debts of persons transferred to those institutions.

5. As a result of the Italian Government's renunciation of its credits towards these institutions, the latter will proceed to the cancellation of the debts of the transferees and to the cancellation of the hypothecs which Is guarantee.

text_fr: Article  X
text_en: Article X

items_fr: 1. II sera  etabli  un  Tribunal  des  Nations  Unies, compose  de  trois  personnalites  choisies  par  le  Secre› taire general pour leur competence  juridique parmi  les nationaux  de  trois  Etats  differents  non  directement interesses.  Le  Tribunal,  qui  se  prononcera  en  droit, aura une  double  fonction:

a) II donnera  aux  Puissances  administrantes,  au Gouvernement  libyen  lorsqu'il aura ete  constitue, ainsi qu'au Gouvernement  italien  les  instructions  que  l'une quelconque  de  ces  autorit~.s  pourra  lui  demander  en vue  de  l'execution de  la  presente  resolution;

b) II  decidera  de  toutes' les  contestations  qui  surgi› raient  entre  lesdites  autorites  au  sujet  de  !'interpreta› tion  et  de l' execution  de  la  presente  resolution.  11 pourra etre  saisi  par  requete  unilaterale.

2. Les  Puissances  administrantes,  le  Gouvernement libyen,  des  qu'il aura  ete  constitue,  ainsi  que  le  Gou› vernement  italien  fourniront  le  plus  tot  possible  au Tribunal des  informations et  l'aide dout ii pourra avoir besom  pour  l'accomplissement de  ses  fonctions.

3. Le Tribunal aura son siege en Libye. Le Tribunal determinera sa  procedure.  II  fournira  aux parties inte› ressees !'occasion d'exposer leurs vues,  et  aura le  droit de  demander, a toute autorite  et a toute  personne qu'il estimera  en  mesure  de  les  lui  donner,  les  renseigne› ments  et  les  temoignages  dont ii aura besoin.  A  defaut d'unanimite, il  se  prononcera  a la  majorite  des  voix. Les  decisions  du  Tribunal  seront  sans  appel  et  obliga› toires&.
items_en: 1. A United Nations Tribunal shall be established, composed of three persons chosen by the Secretary-General for their legal competence among nationals of three different States not directly concerned. The Tribunal, which shall rule in law, shall have a dual function:

(a) He shall instruct the administering Powers, the Government of Libya once it has been constituted, and the Government of Italy, that any such authority may request him to carry out the present resolution;
(b) It shall decide on all disputes which arise between the said authorities concerning the interpretation and implementation of this resolution. 11 may be seized by means of a single request.

2. The administering Powers, the Government of Libya, as constituted, and the Italian Government shall, as soon as possible, provide the Tribunal with information and assistance in the performance of its functions.

3. The Tribunal shall have its seat in Libya. The Tribunal shall determine its procedure. It shall provide the interested parties with the opportunity to present their views, and shall have the right to request, to any authority and to any person whom it considers able to give them, the information and the testimony which it may require. If it fails to do so, it shall rule by majority vote. The decisions of the Tribunal shall be without appeal and shall be binding.

text_fr: 326eme  seance  pleniere, le  15  decembre  1950.
text_en: 326th plenary session, 15 December 1950.

text_fr: B
text_en: B

text_fr: L' Assemblee generale
text_en: The General Assembly

text_fr: Autorise le Secretaire  general,  conformement  aux usages  etablis,
text_en: Authorizes the Secretary General, in accordance with established practice,

items_fr: 1. A prendre des  dispositions  pour que  les  membres du tribunal  etabli a !'article X  ci-dessus  rec;oivent  une remuneration  appropriee  et a les  rembourser  de  leurs frais  de  voyage  et  de  subsistance;

2. A  fournir a ce  tribunal  le  personnel  et les  facili› tes  que  le  Secretaire  general  jugera  necessaires  pour mettre en  reuvre  les  dispositions  de  la  presente  resolu› tion,  employant  autant  que  possible  le  personnel  de  la Mission  des  Nations  Unies  en  Libye.
items_en: 1. To make arrangements for the members of the court established to receive appropriate compensation and to reimburse them for their travel and subsistence expenses;

2. To provide this Tribunal with the personnel and facilities which the Secretary-General considers necessary to implement the provisions of this resolution, employing as far as possible the personnel of the United Nations Mission in Libya.

text_fr: 326eme  seance  pleniere, le  15 decembre  1950.
text_en: 326th plenary session, 15 December 1950.

text_fr: 389 ( V).  A88istance  technique et financiere a la Libye
text_en: 389 (V). A88technical and financial assistance to Libya

text_fr: Considerant que  les  biens  publics  et prives  en  Libye, tant  meubles  qu'immeubles, et le  reseau  de  communi› cations  du  pays  ont  subi,  du  fait  de  la  guerre,  des dommages  considerables,
text_en: Recognizing that the public and private property in Libya, both movable and immovable, and the nation's communications network have suffered considerable damage as a result of the war,

text_fr: Considerant que  ces  dommages  de  guerre,  et  la necessite  de  les  reparer,  representent  l'un des  princi› paux problemes  economiques  et  financiers  dont i1 faut tenir  compte  pour  qu'une Libye  independante  puisse etre  constituee  dans  des  conditions  permettant  de  rea› liser  le  progres  economique  et  social  du  pays,  ce  qui est  l'un  des  objectifs  averes  de  !'Organisation  des Nations  Unies  comme  l'indique le  paragraphe  4  de  la
text_en: Considering that these war damage, and the need to repair it, represent one of the main economic and financial problems which must be taken into account in order for an independent Libya to be established under conditions which would enable the economic and social progress of the country to be achieved, which is one of the objectives of the United Nations, as indicated in paragraph 4 of the

text_fr: resolution  adoptee  le  17  novembre  1950  par  l'Assem› blee  generate 7 ,
text_en: resolution adopted on 17 November 1950 by the Assem› blee generate 7 ,

text_fr: L' Assemblee  generale
text_en: The General Assembly

items_fr: Charge le Secretaire  general  d'etudier le  probleme des  dommages  de  guerre  en  relation  avec  !'assistance technique  et  financiere  que  la  Libye  pourra  demander au  Conseil  economique  et  social,  aux institutions  spe› cialisees  et  au  Secretaire  general;  le  charge  egalement de  faire  rapport a ce  sujet a la  sixieme  session  de I' Assemblee  generate.
items_en: Requests the Secretary General to study the problem of war damage in relation to technical and financial assistance which Libya may request from the Economic and Social Council, the special institutions and the Secretary General; the task also to report thereon to the Sixth Session of the General Assembly.

text_fr: 326eme seance  pleniere, le  15 decembre  1950.
text_en: 326th plenary session, 15 December 1950.

text_fr: 390  (V).  Erythree:  rapport  de  la  CommiHion des Nations  Unies  pour l'Erythree;  rapport de la Commission interimaire de l' Assemblee generale  sur  le  rappor.t  de  la  CommiHion des  Nations  Unies  pour l'Erythree
text_en: 390 (V). Eritrea: report of the United Nations Committee on Eritrea; report of the Interim Committee of the General Assembly on the report of the United Nations Committee on Eritrea

text_fr: A
text_en: A

text_fr: Attendu qu'en  vertu  du  paragraphe  3  de  l'annexe XI du  Traite  de  paix  de  1947  aver.  l'Italie, les  Puis› sances  interessees  sont  convenues  d'accepter la  recom› mandation  de l' Assemblee  generale  sur  le  sort  des anciennes  colonies  italiennes  en  Afrique  et  de  prendre les  mesures  appropriees  pour la  mettre a execution,
text_en: Whereas, pursuant to paragraph 3 of Annex XI of the 1947 Peace Treaty, Italy, the interested parties have agreed to accept the recommendation of the General Assembly on the fate of the former Italian colonies in Africa and to take appropriate measures to implement it,

text_fr: Attendu qu'en  vertu  du  paragraphe  2  de  cette annexe  XI,  le  sort  de  ces  colonies  doit  etre  regle  en tenant  compte  des  aspirations  et du bien-etre des  habi› tants,  ainsi  que  des  exigences  de  la  paix et de  la secu› rite, et  en  prenant  en  consideration  les  vues  des gouvernements  interesses,
text_en: Whereas, in accordance with paragraph 2 of Annex XI, the fate of these colonies must be regulated taking into account the aspirations and well-being of the habi› tants, as well as the demands of peace and security, and taking into account the views of the interested Governments,

text_fr: En consequence,
text_en: As a result,

text_fr: L' Assemblee  generale, tenant  compte  des  rapports 8 de  la  Commission  des  Nations  Unies  pour  l'Erythree et  de  la  Commission  interimaire,  et
text_en: The General Assembly, taking into account reports 8 of the United Nations Commission on Eritrea and the Interim Commission, and

text_fr: Tenant compte
text_en: Taking into account

items_fr: a)  Des  aspirations  et  du  bien-etre  des  habitants  de l'Erythree ainsi  que  des  opinions  des  differents  grou› pements  raciaux,  religieux  et politiques  des  provinces du  territoire et de  !'aptitude de  la  population a se gou› verner  elle-meme ;
b)  Des  interets  de  la  paix  et  de  la  securite  en Afrique orientate ;

c) Des  droits  et  des  revendications  de  l'Ethiopie, motives  par  des  considerations  geographiques,  histo› riques,  ethniques  ou  economiques,  et tout specialement du  besoin  legitime  de  l'Ethiopie d'avoir un acces  suffi› sant a la  mer,
items_en: (a) The aspirations and well-being of the inhabitants of Eritrea, as well as the opinions of the different racial, religious and political groups in the provinces of the Territory and the ability of the population to see themselves;
(b) The interests of peace and security in Eastern Africa;
(c) Ethiopia's rights and claims, motivated by geographical, historical, ethnic or economic considerations, and especially Ethiopia's legitimate need for adequate access to the sea;

text_fr: Prenant  en  consideration qu'il importe  de  faire  en sorte  que  les  communautes  etrangeres  continuent a collaborer au developpement economique de  l'Erythree,
text_en: Taking into account the importance of ensuring that foreign communities continue to cooperate in the economic development of Eritrea,

text_fr: Reconnaissant que le  reglement du sort de  l'Erythree doit  avoir  pour  base  l'etroite association  politique  et economique  de  celle-ci  avec  l'Ethiopie, et
text_en: Recognizing that the settlement of the fate of Eritrea must be based on its close political and economic association with Ethiopia, and
